highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.101 Released
highlights.summary: The latest release adds a `/team-onboarding` command that automatically generates a ramp-up guide for new teammates based on your local Claude Code usage history. It also adds OS CA certificate store trust by default, meaning enterprise TLS proxies should now work out of the box without extra configuration.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.98 Released
highlights.summary: This release introduces an interactive setup wizard for Google Vertex AI, accessible from the login screen under '3rd-party platform', walking you through GCP authentication, project and region selection, and model pinning. It also adds a `CLAUDE_CODE_PERFORCE_MODE` environment variable for teams using Perforce version control.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.97 Released
highlights.summary: A focus view toggle (`Ctrl+O`) is now available in `NO_FLICKER` mode, giving you a clean view of your prompt, a one-line tool summary with diff stats, and the final response. The update also adds a `refreshInterval` setting for the status line and exposes `workspace.git_worktree` for better multi-worktree workflow support.

highlights.title: Depwire – Codebase Dependency Graph and MCP Server for AI Coding Assistants
highlights.summary: Depwire is an open-source tool that generates a dependency graph of your codebase and exposes it as an MCP server, giving AI coding assistants like Claude Code richer context about how your code is structured. This can help Claude make more accurate suggestions by understanding module relationships before editing files.

highlights.title: Show HN: Give Your AI Coding Assistant Persistent, Semantic Memory
highlights.summary: Total Recall is an open-source project that adds persistent, semantic memory to AI coding assistants, so Claude Code can remember project context, decisions, and patterns across sessions. This addresses one of the most common pain points beginners face: re-explaining your project setup every time you start a new conversation.

highlights.title: Reallocating $100/Month Claude Code Spend to Zed and OpenRouter
highlights.summary: A developer shares their experience switching from a $100/month Claude Code subscription to a combination of Zed editor and OpenRouter to reduce costs and gain model flexibility. This is a useful read for anyone evaluating their AI tooling budget and wanting to understand the trade-offs between Claude Code's integrated experience and more open setups.

highlights.title: Claude Code Complete Guide (0 to 1): MCP, SubAgent, Agent Skill, Hook, Images, Context, Background Tasks
highlights.summary: This comprehensive Bilibili tutorial covers Claude Code from initial installation through advanced topics like MCP integration, sub-agents, hooks, image handling, and background task management, with clear timestamps for easy navigation. It is an excellent structured resource for beginners who want a single video walkthrough of all major Claude Code features.

highlights.title: Issue: Claude Code Is Unusable for Complex Engineering Tasks After February Updates
highlights.summary: A widely discussed GitHub issue reports that recent updates have significantly degraded Claude Code's ability to handle complex, multi-step engineering tasks, with users noting regressions in code quality and agentic reliability. If you've noticed similar problems, following this issue is a good way to track official responses and community workarounds.

tips_of_the_day: Use the new `/team-onboarding` command before a teammate joins your project — it reads your actual Claude Code usage history to produce a tailored guide, saving you hours of documentation writing.

Store key project decisions, architecture notes, and recurring context in a `CLAUDE.md` file at the root of your repo. Claude Code reads this file automatically at the start of each session, acting as a lightweight form of persistent memory without any extra tooling.

If Claude Code gives inconsistent results on a complex task, try breaking it into smaller, explicit subtasks using Plan Mode first. Reviewing and approving the plan before execution gives you a natural checkpoint to catch misunderstandings early.
